records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.452 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Whoever walks with a funeral procession until the funeral prayer is offered and then returns will receive one qirat (measure of reward). If they continue walking with it until the deceased is buried, they will receive two qirats, and one qirat is like the size of Mount Uhud."
records.arabicText: 452 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ مَشَى مَعَ جَنَازَةٍ حَتَّى يُصَلَّى عَلَيْهَا ثُمَّ رَجَعَ كَانَ لَهُ قِيرَاطٌ وَ إِذَا مَشَى مَعَهَا حَتَّى تُدْفَنَ كَانَ لَهُ قِيرَاطَانِ وَ اَلْقِيرَاطُ مِثْلُ جَبَلِ أُحُدٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.452 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever walks with a funeral procession until the funeral prayer is offered and then returns will receive one qirat (measure of reward). If they continue walking with it until the deceased is buried, they will receive two qirats, and one qirat is like the size of Mount Uhud."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.453 - Imam (as) said: "Whoever follows the funeral procession of a Muslim will be granted four intercessions on the Day of Judgment. Whatever supplication or statement they make, the angel will say to them, 'And for you is the same.'"
records.arabicText: 453 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ تَبِعَ جَنَازَةَ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أُعْطِيَ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ أَرْبَعَ شَفَاعَاتٍ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ شَيْئاً إِلاَّ قَالَ لَهُ اَلْمَلَكُ وَ لَكَ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever follows the funeral procession of a Muslim will be granted four intercessions on the Day of Judgment. Whatever supplication or statement they make, the angel will say to them, 'And for you is the same.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.454 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever takes hold of the four sides of the bier (funeral bier) will have forty major sins forgiven by Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 454 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ أَخَذَ بِجَوَانِبِ اَلسَّرِيرِ اَلْأَرْبَعَةِ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ أَرْبَعِينَ كَبِيرَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.454 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever takes hold of the four sides of the bier (funeral bier) will have forty major sins forgiven by Allah (swt)."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.455 - Imam (as) said: "Whoever accompanies the funeral of a believer until they are buried in their grave, Allah (swt) will assign seventy angels to accompany and seek forgiveness for that person when they rise from their grave to stand at the gathering (on the Day of Judgment)."
records.arabicText: 455 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ شَيَّعَ جَنَازَةَ مُؤْمِنٍ حَتَّى يُدْفَنَ فِي قَبْرِهِ وَكَّلَ اَللَّهُ بِهِ سَبْعِينَ مَلَكاً مِنَ اَلْمُشَيِّعِينَ يُشَيِّعُونَهُ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لَهُ إِذَا خَرَجَ مِنْ قَبْرِهِ إِلَى اَلْمَوْقِفِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.456 - Imam (as) said:
"The first gift given to a believer in their grave is the forgiveness granted to those who followed their funeral procession."
records.arabicText: 456 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ مَا يُتْحَفُ بِهِ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ فِي قَبْرِهِ أَنْ يُغْفَرَ لِمَنْ تَبِعَ جَنَازَتَهُ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.457 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"When a believer enters their grave, it is proclaimed: 'Behold, your first gift is Paradise! And the first gift for those who followed your funeral procession is forgiveness.'"
records.arabicText: 457 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا دَخَلَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ قَبْرَهُ نُودِيَ أَلاَ إِنَّ أَوَّلَ حِبَائِكَ اَلْجَنَّةُ ، أَلاَ وَ أَوَّلُ حِبَاءِ مَنْ تَبِعَكَ اَلْمَغْفِرَةُ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.458 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said: “Whoever carries their deceased brother on all four sides of the bier, Allah (swt) will erase forty major sins from them."
records.arabicText: 458 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ حَمَلَ أَخَاهُ اَلْمَيِّتَ بِجَوَانِبِ اَلسَّرِيرِ اَلْأَرْبَعَةِ مَحَا اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَرْبَعِينَ كَبِيرَةً مِنَ اَلْكَبَائِرِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.459 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Whoever takes hold of the handles of the bier, Allah (swt) will forgive twenty-five major sins for them. And if they carry it on all four sides, they will be cleansed of their sins."
records.arabicText: 459 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ أَخَذَ بِقَوَائِمِ اَلسَّرِيرِ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ خَمْساً وَ عِشْرِينَ كَبِيرَةً وَ إِذَا رَبَّعَ خَرَجَ مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.459 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever takes hold of the handles of the bier, Allah (swt) will forgive twenty-five major sins for them. And if they carry it on all four sides, they will be cleansed of their sins."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.460 - Imam (as) said to Ishaq ibn Ammar:
"When you carry the sides of the bier of the deceased, you will be cleansed of your sins as if you were just born from your mother."
records.arabicText: 460 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِإِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ إِذَا حَمَلْتَ جَوَانِبَ اَلسَّرِيرِ سَرِيرِ اَلْمَيِّتِ خَرَجْتَ مِنَ اَلذُّنُوبِ كَمَا وَلَدَتْكَ أُمُّكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.460 - Imam (as) said to Ishaq ibn Ammar:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When you carry the sides of the bier of the deceased, you will be cleansed of your sins as if you were just born from your mother."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.461 - Abu Ja'far, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"Walking behind the funeral procession is better than walking in front of it, but there is no harm if you walk in front."
records.arabicText: 461 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَلْمَشْيَ خَلْفَ اَلْجَنَازَةِ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ اَلْمَشْيِ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهَا وَ لاَ بَأْسَ إِنْ مَشَيْتَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.461 - Abu Ja'far, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Walking behind the funeral procession is better than walking in front of it, but there is no harm if you walk in front."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.462 - Hussein ibn Saeed wrote to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), asking about the bier of the deceased: Is there a specific side that should be carried first from among its four sides, or can one carry it from any side that is easiest?
Imam (as) replied: "From whichever side he wishes."
records.arabicText: 462 - وَ كَتَبَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ إِلَى أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَسْأَلُهُ: عَنْ سَرِيرِ اَلْمَيِّتِ يُحْمَلُ أَ لَهُ جَانِبٌ يُبْدَأُ بِهِ فِي اَلْحَمْلِ مِنْ جَوَانِبِهِ اَلْأَرْبَعَةِ أَوْ مَا خَفَّ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْمِلُ مِنْ أَيِّ اَلْجَوَانِبِ شَاءَ فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «مِنْ أَيِّهَا شَاءَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.462 - Hussein ibn Saeed wrote to Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), asking about the bier of the deceased: Is there a specific side that should be carried first from among its four sides, or can one carry it from any side that is easiest?
Imam (as) replied:

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.463 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a funeral procession in which fire (torches or lamps) is carried.
Imam (as) replied: "The daughter of the Messenger of Allah (sw) was taken out at night, and lamps accompanied her."
records.arabicText: 463 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْجَنَازَةِ يُخْرَجُ مَعَهَا بِالنَّارِ فَقَالَ «إِنَّ اِبْنَةَ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أُخْرِجَ بِهَا لَيْلاً وَ مَعَهَا مَصَابِيحُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.463 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a funeral procession in which fire (torches or lamps) is carried.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The daughter of the Messenger of Allah (sw) was taken out at night, and lamps accompanied her."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.464 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of them (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) or Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) ) that he asked about walking with the funeral procession.
Imam (as) replied: "In front of it, on its right side, on its left side, and behind it."
records.arabicText: 464 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ - سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَشْيِ مَعَ اَلْجَنَازَةِ فَقَالَ «بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَ عَنْ يَمِينِهَا وَ عَنْ شِمَالِهَا وَ خَلْفِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.464 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of them (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) or Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) ) that he asked about walking with the funeral procession.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "In front of it, on its right side, on its left side, and behind it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.465 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"When Adam (as) passed away and it was time to perform the prayer for him, the son of Adam, Hibatullah (Seth), said to Archangel Jibril (as):
'Advance, O’ Messenger (intermediary) of Allah (swt), and lead the prayer over the Prophet of Allah (swt).'
Archangel Jibril (as) replied: 'Allah (swt), the Exalted, commanded us to prostrate to your father, and we do not go ahead of the best of his progeny. You are among the most righteous of them, so you should lead.'
Hibatullah advanced and performed five takbirs over him, corresponding to the number of prayers that Allah (swt) made obligatory for the Ummah of Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon
records.arabicText: 465 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «لَمَّا مَاتَ آدَمُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَبَلَغَ إِلَى اَلصَّلاَةِ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ هِبَةُ اَللَّهِ لِجَبْرَئِيلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ تَقَدَّمْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَصَلِّ عَلَى نَبِيِّ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ جَبْرَئِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَمَرَنَا بِالسُّجُودِ لِأَبِيكَ فَلَسْنَا نَتَقَدَّمُ أَبْرَارَ وُلْدِهِ وَ أَنْتَ مِنْ أَبَرِّهِمْ فَتَقَدَّمَ فَكَبَّرَ عَلَيْهِ خَمْساً عِدَّةَ اَلصَّلَوَاتِ اَلَّتِي فَرَضَهَا اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَلَى أُمَّةِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ هِيَ اَلسُّنَّةُ اَلْجَارِيَةُ فِي وُلْدِهِ إِلَى يَوْمِ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.465 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When Adam (as) passed away and it was time to perform the prayer for him, the son of Adam, Hibatullah (Seth), said to Archangel Jibril (as):
'Advance, O’ Messenger (intermediary) of Allah (swt), and lead the prayer over the Prophet of Allah (swt).'
Archangel Jibril (as) replied: 'Allah (swt), the Exalted, commanded us to prostrate to your father, and we do not go ahead of the best of his progeny. You are among the most righteous of them, so you should lead.'
Hibatullah advanced and performed five takbirs over him, corresponding to the number of prayers that Allah (swt) made obligatory for the Ummah of Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.466 - When the Messenger of Allah (sw) offered the funeral prayer over a deceased person, he would recite the first takbir and then recite the testimony of faith (shahada).
He would then recite the second takbir, send blessings upon the Prophet and his family, and make supplications.
Upon the third takbir, he would pray for the believing men and women.
After the fourth takbir, he would supplicate for the deceased,
And then conclude with a fifth takbir before leaving.
When Allah (swt), the Exalted, forbade him from praying over the hypocrites,
he recited the first takbir, recited the shahada,
then recited the second takbir, sent blessings upon the Prophet and his family,
recited the third takbir, and prayed for the believing men and women.
Upon the fourth takbir, he did not offer any supplication for the deceased and then concluded.
records.arabicText: 466 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا صَلَّى عَلَى مَيِّتٍ كَبَّرَ فَتَشَهَّدَ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ فَصَلَّى عَلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ وَ آلِهِ وَ دَعَا ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ وَ دَعَا لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ اَلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ اَلرَّابِعَةَ وَ دَعَا لِلْمَيِّتِ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ وَ اِنْصَرَفَ فَلَمَّا نَهَاهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ عَلَى اَلْمُنَافِقِينَ فَكَبَّرَ وَ تَشَهَّدَ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ فَصَلَّى عَلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ وَ آلِهِ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ وَ دَعَا لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ اَلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ ثُمَّ كَبَّرَ اَلرَّابِعَةَ وَ اِنْصَرَفَ فَلَمْ يَدْعُ لِلْمَيِّتِ.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
When the Messenger of Allah (sw) offered the funeral prayer over a deceased person, he would recite the first takbir and then recite the testimony of faith (shahada).
He would then recite the second takbir, send blessings upon the Prophet and his family, and make supplications.
Upon the third takbir, he would pray for the believing men and women.
After the fourth takbir, he would supplicate for the deceased,
And then conclude with a fifth takbir before leaving.
When Allah (swt), the Exalted, forbade him from praying over the hypocrites,
he recited the first takbir, recited the shahada,
then recited the second takbir, sent blessings upon the Prophet and his family,
recited the third takbir, and prayed for the believing men and women.
Upon the fourth takbir, he did not offer any supplication for the deceased and then concluded.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.467 - It has been narrated that the reason behind this practice is that Allah (swt), the Exalted, has prescribed five obligatory prayers for people, and thus, one takbir is designated for the deceased from each of these obligatory prayers.
records.arabicText: 467 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ اَلْعِلَّةَ فِي ذَلِكَ أَنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى فَرَضَ عَلَى اَلنَّاسِ خَمْسَ صَلَوَاتٍُ فَجَعَلَ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ فَرِيضَةٍ لِلْمَيِّتِ تَكْبِيرَةً.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.468 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) recited seventy takbirs (Allahu Akbar) over Hamza.
records.arabicText: 468 - وَ كَبَّرَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَلَى حَمْزَةَ سَبْعِينَ تَكْبِيرَةً.
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (sw) recited seventy takbirs (Allahu Akbar) over Hamza.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.469 - Imam Ali (as) recited twenty-five takbirs (Allahu Akbar) over Sahl ibn Hunayf.
records.arabicText: 469 - وَ كَبَّرَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَى سَهْلِ بْنِ حُنَيْفٍ خَمْساً وَ عِشْرِينَ تَكْبِيرَةً.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Ali (as) recited twenty-five takbirs (Allahu Akbar) over Sahl ibn Hunayf.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.470 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to recite takbirs five at a time over the deceased. When people would arrive late and say, 'O’ Commander of the Faithful, we did not catch the prayer over Sahl ibn Hunayf,' he would place the bier down and recite five takbirs over him again, repeating this process until he reached the grave, doing so five times”.
records.arabicText: 470 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُكَبِّرُ خَمْساً خَمْساً كَانَ إِذَا أَدْرَكَهُ اَلنَّاسُ قَالُوا يَا أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَمْ نُدْرِكِ اَلصَّلاَةَ عَلَى سَهْلِ بْنِ حُنَيْفٍ فَيَضَعُهُ فَيُكَبِّرُ عَلَيْهِ خَمْساً حَتَّى اِنْتَهَى إِلَى قَبْرِهِ خَمْسَ مَرَّاتٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.470 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to recite takbirs five at a time over the deceased. When people would arrive late and say, 'O’ Commander of the Faithful, we did not catch the prayer over Sahl ibn Hunayf,' he would place the bier down and recite five takbirs over him again, repeating this process until he reached the grave, doing so five times”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.471 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"If a person catches one or two takbirs of the funeral prayer over the deceased, they should complete the remaining takbirs consecutively."
records.arabicText: 471 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «إِذَا أَدْرَكَ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلتَّكْبِيرَةَ وَ اَلتَّكْبِيرَتَيْنِ مِنَ اَلصَّلاَةِ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ فَلْيَقْضِ مَا بَقِيَ مُتَتَابِعاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.471 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a person catches one or two takbirs of the funeral prayer over the deceased, they should complete the remaining takbirs consecutively."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.472 - Omar ibn Yazid narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that He said: "If a believer passes away and forty men from among the believers attend their funeral and say:
Allāhumma innā lā na‘lam minhu illā khayran wa anta a‘lamu bihi minnā
'O’ Allah (swt), we do not know of anything but good from this person, and You know him better than we do,'
Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, responds, 'I have accepted your testimony and forgiven him for what I know that you do not.'"
records.arabicText: 472 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا مَاتَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ فَحَضَرَ جَنَازَتَهُ أَرْبَعُونَ رَجُلاً مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ قَالُوا: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا لاَ نَعْلَمُ مِنْهُ إِلاَّ خَيْراً وَ أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ مِنَّا قَالَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى قَدْ أَجَزْتُ شَهَادَاتِكُمْ وَ غَفَرْتُ لَهُ مَا عَلِمْتُ مِمَّا لاَ تَعْلَمُونَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.472 - Omar ibn Yazid narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a believer passes away and forty men from among the believers attend their funeral and say:
Allāhumma innā lā na‘lam minhu illā khayran wa anta a‘lamu bihi minnā
'O’ Allah (swt), we do not know of anything but good from this person, and You know him better than we do,'
Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, responds, 'I have accepted your testimony and forgiven him for what I know that you do not.'"

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.473 - Al-Fadl ibn Abd al-Malik asked Imam (as): "Is it permissible to perform the funeral prayer for the deceased in the mosque?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes”.
records.arabicText: 473 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْفَضْلُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ: هَلْ يُصَلَّى عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.474 - Abu Basir asked Imam (as) about who has the most right to perform the funeral prayer for a woman who has passed away.
Imam (as) replied: "Her husband."
Abu Basir then asked: "Does the husband have more right than her father, son, or brother?"
The Imam said: "Yes, and he may also wash her."
[AL SADUQ]
My father wrote in his message to me:
"Know, my son, that the person with the most right to lead the funeral prayer for the deceased is whoever is designated by the deceased's guardian (Wali). If there is a man from the Banu Hashim among the people, he has priority for leading the prayer if designated by the guardian of the deceased. If someone leads without being appointed by the deceased’s guardian, then they are usurping that right."
records.arabicText:
474 - وَ سَأَلَهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ: عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَمُوتُ مَنْ أَحَقُّ بِالصَّلاَةِ عَلَيْهَا قَالَ «زَوْجُهَا» فَقَالَ لَهُ اَلزَّوْجُ أَحَقُّ مِنَ اَلْأَبِ وَ اَلْوَلَدِ وَ اَلْأَخِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ يُغَسِّلُهَا.
وقال أبي رحمه الله في رسالته إلي: إعلم يا بني أن أولى الناس بالصلاة على الميت من يقدمه ولي الميت، وإن كان في القوم رجل من بني هاشم فهو أحق بالصلاة عليه إذا قدمه ولي الميت، فان تقدم من غير أن يقدمه ولي الميت فهو غاصب.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.475 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If you miss the funeral prayer for the deceased until they are buried, there is no problem in performing the prayer for them after they have been buried”.
records.arabicText: 475 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا فَاتَتْكَ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ حَتَّى يُدْفَنَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَيْهِ وَ قَدْ دُفِنَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.475 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you miss the funeral prayer for the deceased until they are buried, there is no problem in performing the prayer for them after they have been buried”.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.476 - "The Messenger of Allah (sw), if he missed the funeral prayer for a deceased person, would offer the prayer at their grave”.
records.arabicText: 476 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا فَاتَتْهُ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ صَلَّى عَلَى قَبْرِهِ.
